mcqs.question: What input units should the Force Calculator accept for mass and acceleration?
options: Only kilograms (kg) for mass and meters per second squared (m/s²) for acceleration.
Kilograms (kg) and grams (g) for mass; meters per second squared (m/s²) and centimeters per second squared (cm/s²) for acceleration.
Allow users to select between metric (kg, m/s²) and imperial (lb, ft/s²) unit sets.
Automatically detect and convert common units entered directly into text fields (e.g., "10lb", "5cm/s^2").

mcqs.question: In what unit should the calculated force (F) be displayed to the user?
options: Newtons (N) only.
Dyne (dyn) only.
Pounds-force (lbf) only.
Allow users to select between Newtons (N) and Pounds-force (lbf).

mcqs.question: How should the system handle non-numeric characters (e.g., "abc", "10kg") entered into the mass or acceleration input fields?
options: Display an error message and prevent calculation until all non-numeric characters are removed.
Automatically strip non-numeric characters, attempting to parse only the numerical part (e.g., "10kg" becomes "10").
Default the field to '0' if any non-numeric characters are present, then proceed with calculation.
Highlight the invalid input field in red and disable the calculation button.

mcqs.question: Should the calculator allow negative values for mass or acceleration?
options: Allow negative values for both, calculating a negative force if applicable.
Disallow negative mass, but allow negative acceleration (e.g., for deceleration).
Disallow all negative inputs, requiring positive values for both mass and acceleration.
Treat any negative input as its absolute value (e.g., -5 becomes 5) before calculation.

mcqs.question: What level of precision should the calculated force (F) be displayed with?
options: Always round to two decimal places.
Display up to four decimal places, rounding only if necessary.
Use "significant figures" based on the precision of the input values.
Allow the user to select their desired output precision (e.g., 1, 2, or 3 decimal places).

mcqs.question: How should the calculation be triggered after the user enters values?
options: Automatically calculate and update the result in real-time as values are typed.
Require the user to click a dedicated "Calculate" button.
Calculate automatically when both input fields contain valid numbers and the user unfocuses from the last input field.
Calculate automatically on pressing 'Enter' in either input field.

mcqs.question: What is the preferred user interface for inputting mass and acceleration values?
options: Simple text input fields with no associated unit selectors.
Text input fields with separate dropdowns for unit selection (e.g., kg, g, lb).
Text input fields with a single global unit system selector (e.g., 'Metric' or 'Imperial').
Sliders for rough estimation, supplemented by text input fields for precise values.

mcqs.question: What should be the initial state of the mass and acceleration input fields when the page loads?
options: Both fields should be completely empty.
Both fields should default to '0'.
Both fields should default to '1' (e.g., 1 kg, 1 m/s²).
Display placeholder text (e.g., "Enter Mass", "Enter Acceleration") in empty fields.
